reason: The remediation only edits /etc/selinux/config and runs the standard fixfiles relabeling, both of which are common STIG actions. While enforcing SELinux can cause services to fail if policies are incomplete, the fix includes a relabel step and a reboot, minimizing the chance of immediate lockout. Therefore it is considered safe to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential service disruption if SELinux policies are not fully compatible; Temporary loss of functionality until reboot completes
